title: MET B2 Vocabulary Interactive Exercise Bank
subtitle: Upper-intermediate vocabulary practice for common MET topic areas

targetAudience.learnerProfile: General English learners and healthcare/professional learners preparing for MET

platformMapping.true_false_with_explanation: true/false toggle plus explanation text box

teacherNotes.mainUse: Use these exercises after teaching the vocabulary lists or as platform homework.
teacherNotes.recommendedFlow: Start with matching or fill-in-the-blank, then move to scenario and short-answer tasks.
teacherNotes.assessmentSuggestion: For short-answer/scenario items, evaluate clarity, vocabulary accuracy, grammar control, and support/detail.
commonMistakesToWatch: Using target words with the wrong part of speech
Listing vocabulary without a clear sentence meaning
Giving opinions without reasons or examples
Overusing simple words when a B2 word would be more precise

modules.title: Health and Lifestyle

targetVocabulary.definition: to keep something in good condition or at the same level
including the right amount of different things
a regular way of doing things
to stop something from happening
to make something smaller or less serious
to make something better
causing damage or problems
a good result or advantage
related to the body
related to the mind
moving often or doing many activities
not good for your health
the state of someone’s health or body
to become healthy again after illness or injury
to give someone support or confidence to do something
to stay away from something
containing substances that help the body stay healthy
the way a person lives
general health and happiness
too much or more than necessary

learningObjectives: Use B2 vocabulary to discuss healthy habits and well-being.
Explain benefits, risks, and lifestyle choices using reasons and examples.
Recognize vocabulary in MET-style everyday and opinion contexts.

exercises.title: Healthy Habits in Context
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one word from the word bank.

items.sentence: Regular exercise can help people ______ a healthy weight.
items.explanation: 'Maintain' means keep something at the same level or in good condition.

items.sentence: A ______ breakfast gives the body energy for the morning.
items.explanation: 'Nutritious' describes food that is good for the body.

items.sentence: Good sleep can improve mental ______.
items.explanation: 'Well-being' means general health and happiness.

items.sentence: Doctors often encourage healthy habits to ______ future illness.
items.explanation: 'Prevent' means stop something from happening.

items.sentence: ______ screen time may affect sleep quality.
items.explanation: 'Excessive' means too much or more than necessary.

exercises.title: Benefits and Risks
exercises.instructions: Choose all correct answers. More than one answer may be correct.

items.prompt: Which words can describe positive health results?
items.explanation: 'Benefit' and 'well-being' are positive. 'Harmful' and 'unhealthy' describe negative effects.

items.prompt: Which actions can help improve lifestyle?
items.explanation: Avoiding too much sugar and maintaining a routine are healthy actions. The other options are negative.

exercises.title: MET-Style Opinion: Health
exercises.instructions: Answer in 3–5 sentences. Use at least three target vocabulary words.
exercises.prompt: What is one habit people should develop to improve their health? Explain your answer with a reason and an example.
exercises.modelResponse: People should develop a regular exercise routine because it supports both physical and mental well-being. For example, walking for thirty minutes after work can reduce stress and help people stay active. This habit is simple, but it can have a strong long-term benefit.

modules.title: Workplace Communication

targetVocabulary.definition: a person you work with
a person who manages workers
the final time or date to finish something
a duty or task you are expected to do
a piece of work that needs to be done
working well without wasting time or energy
to work together with others
to make something easier to understand
something you ask for politely or formally
to answer or react to something
a problem or topic that needs attention
to solve a problem
new information or to add new information
comments about someone’s work or performance
an official way of doing something
a period of work time
to share information, ideas, or feelings
able to be trusted or depended on
suitable for a work environment
to decide what is most important

learningObjectives: Use professional vocabulary for workplace situations.
Identify appropriate language for problems, updates, feedback, and priorities.
Practice polite and clear workplace communication.

exercises.title: Workplace Word Match
exercises.instructions: Match each word to the correct definition.

pairs.definition: the final date or time to finish something
pairs.explanation: A deadline is connected to time management.

pairs.definition: a person who manages workers
pairs.explanation: A supervisor gives instructions and checks work.

pairs.definition: to make something clearer
pairs.explanation: You clarify when information is confusing.

pairs.definition: comments that help someone improve
pairs.explanation: Feedback explains what was good and what should change.

pairs.definition: to decide what is most important
pairs.explanation: People prioritize when they cannot do everything at once.

exercises.title: Professional Response Scenario
exercises.instructions: Read the situation and write a response. Use clear, polite, B2-level English.
exercises.scenario: Your supervisor asks why a report was not finished by the deadline. You had a technical issue, but you also forgot to update your colleague.
exercises.task: Write a professional response explaining the issue and what you will do next.
exercises.modelResponse: I apologize for missing the deadline. There was a technical issue with the system, but I should have communicated the problem earlier. I will update my colleague now, prioritize the report, and send the final version by the end of the shift.
exercises.explanation: The model response gives a clear statement, reason, and support. It also uses topic vocabulary naturally instead of listing words randomly.

exercises.title: Workplace Meaning Check
exercises.instructions: Decide if each statement is true or false. Then explain your answer in one short sentence.

items.statement: If a person is reliable, coworkers can usually depend on them.
items.explanation: Correct. A reliable person can be trusted to do what they promise.

items.statement: To resolve an issue means to make it more confusing.
items.explanation: Incorrect meaning. 'Resolve' means find a solution.

items.statement: A request is usually more polite than a demand.
items.modelExplanation: A request is something you ask for politely.
items.explanation: Correct. Requests are generally polite or formal.

modules.title: Education and Learning

targetVocabulary.definition: a piece of schoolwork or work task
a formal talk given to teach people about a subject
to take part in an activity
to make something better
related to school, college, or study
an ability to do something well
a plan used to achieve a goal
to judge or measure the quality of something
something that is needed or demanded
able to do things without much help
something difficult that tests your ability
improvement over time
to give full attention to something
careful study to discover information
to arrange ideas or things clearly
connected to the topic or situation
successful in producing a good result
a way of doing something
to successfully reach a goal
a statement that makes something clear

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary related to studying, courses, and progress.
Explain learning strategies and academic challenges.
Build MET-style answers about education and personal development.

exercises.title: Education Vocabulary in Context
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer.

items.prompt: A useful reading ______ is to look for the main idea before reading every detail.
items.explanation: 'Strategy' means a plan used to reach a goal.

items.prompt: Students should ______ in class discussions to practice speaking.
items.explanation: 'Participate' means take part in an activity.

items.prompt: The teacher gave a clear ______ of the grammar rule.
items.explanation: 'Explanation' means a statement that makes something clear.

exercises.title: Organize a MET Writing Answer
exercises.instructions: Put the sentences in the best logical order.

items.prompt: Order this answer about online learning.

sentences.text: For example, working adults can study at night after their shifts.
Online learning can be effective for many students.
This flexibility helps independent learners make progress.
One reason is that students can choose a convenient schedule.

items.explanation: The best order is statement, reason, example, conclusion.

exercises.title: Education Quick Recall
exercises.instructions: Answer each item quickly. Suggested time: 20 seconds per item.

items.prompt: Word for a piece of schoolwork:
items.explanation: An assignment is schoolwork or a task.

items.prompt: Word for improvement over time:
items.explanation: Progress means improvement over time.

items.prompt: Verb meaning to judge or measure quality:
items.explanation: Evaluate means judge or measure quality.

items.prompt: Adjective meaning connected to the topic:
items.explanation: Relevant means connected to the topic.

modules.title: Technology in Work and Study

targetVocabulary.definition: a machine or tool, especially electronic
the ability to use or enter something
related to computers or electronic technology
easy and useful for a situation
able to be trusted or depended on
working well without wasting time or energy
the right to keep personal information secret
new information or to add new information
a website or system used for a specific purpose
to share information, ideas, or feelings
working by itself without direct human control
to take the place of something or someone
a good point or benefit
a problem or negative point
something that takes your attention away
facts or knowledge about something
something useful that people can use
protection from danger, crime, or data loss
existing online instead of in person
to join or communicate with someone or something

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary about digital tools, online learning, and privacy.
Compare advantages and disadvantages of technology.
Explain how technology affects work and study.

exercises.title: Technology at Work and Study
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one word from the word bank.

items.sentence: The school uses an online ______ for homework and feedback.
items.explanation: A platform is a website or system used for a specific purpose.

items.sentence: Students need a ______ internet connection for online classes.
items.explanation: 'Reliable' means able to be trusted or depended on.

items.sentence: Social media can be a serious ______ while studying.
items.explanation: A distraction takes your attention away.

items.sentence: Hospitals must protect patient ______ when using digital tools.
items.explanation: Privacy is the right to keep personal information secret.

items.sentence: A ______ meeting happens online, not in person.
items.explanation: Virtual means existing online instead of physically.

exercises.title: Technology: Advantage or Problem?
exercises.instructions: Decide if each statement is true or false. Then explain your answer in one short sentence.

items.statement: A disadvantage is a negative point or problem.
items.modelExplanation: A disadvantage is something bad about a choice.
items.explanation: Correct. It contrasts with advantage.

items.statement: Security is not important when people use online platforms.
items.modelExplanation: Security protects people and data online.
items.explanation: False. Security is very important for digital systems.

items.statement: A device can be a phone, tablet, or computer.
items.modelExplanation: A device is an electronic tool or machine.
items.explanation: Correct. These are common examples of devices.

exercises.title: MET-Style Opinion: Technology
exercises.instructions: Answer in 3–5 sentences. Use at least three target vocabulary words.
exercises.prompt: Has technology made studying easier or more difficult? Explain both sides and give your opinion.
exercises.modelResponse: Technology has made studying easier in many ways because students have quick access to resources, videos, and virtual classes. However, it can also create distractions, especially when students use social media during study time. In my opinion, technology is helpful if learners use reliable platforms and manage their time carefully.
successCriteria: Uses at least three technology words

modules.title: Community and Transportation

targetVocabulary.definition: a group of people living in the same area or sharing something
an area of a town or city where people live
ways of moving people or goods from one place to another
cars, buses, and other vehicles on the road
to travel regularly between home and work or school
a place, building, or service used for a specific purpose
available for everyone to use
to make something better
to make something larger or more complete
a person who lives in a particular place
related to a particular area or community
protection from danger or harm
full of too many people or things
a way to solve a problem
the process of building something
a person walking, especially near roads
the way from one place to another
easy to reach, use, or enter
related to cities

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary for city life, transportation, and local problems.
Discuss solutions to community issues.
Practice giving opinions about public decisions.

exercises.title: City Problems and Solutions
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer.

items.prompt: Many ______ complained that the road construction was too noisy.
items.explanation: Residents are people who live in a particular area.

items.prompt: The city wants to ______ the subway system to reach more neighborhoods.
items.explanation: 'Expand' means make something larger or more complete.

items.prompt: More street lights could improve ______ at night.
items.explanation: Safety means protection from danger or harm.

exercises.title: Public Decision Scenario
exercises.instructions: Read the situation and write a response. Use clear, polite, B2-level English.
exercises.scenario: Your city government wants to build a new bus route near your neighborhood. Some residents support it, but others worry about noise and construction.
exercises.task: Write a short response to a city official. Say if you support the plan and explain why.
exercises.modelResponse: I support the new bus route because it would make transportation more accessible for residents who do not drive. Although construction may cause temporary noise, the long-term benefit is stronger than the inconvenience. A better public route could reduce traffic and help the whole community.
exercises.explanation: The model response gives a clear statement, reason, and support. It also uses topic vocabulary naturally instead of listing words randomly.

exercises.title: Problem-Solution Order
exercises.instructions: Put the sentences in the best logical order.

items.prompt: Order this paragraph about traffic.

sentences.text: For example, more buses would give residents an alternative to driving.
One solution is to improve public transportation.
Traffic is a serious problem in many urban areas.
As a result, roads could become less crowded during rush hour.

items.explanation: The answer moves from problem to solution, example, and result.

modules.title: Food, Family, Restaurants, and Routines

targetVocabulary.definition: food eaten at a regular time
made at home instead of bought from a store
one of the foods used to make a dish
the amount of food served to one person
containing substances that help the body stay healthy
the feeling of wanting to eat
the food a person usually eats
a custom or habit passed from one generation to another
to come together in one place
to make something ready
to give food or drinks to someone
to suggest something because it is good
the taste of food or drink
relaxed and informal
the feeling or mood of a place
an arrangement to keep a table, room, or seat for someone
a regular way of doing things
something you do often, usually without thinking
a special time or event
a choice or liking for one thing more than another

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary about meals, routines, restaurants, and traditions.
Describe personal experiences clearly.
Compare preferences using reasons and examples.

exercises.title: Food and Routine Match
exercises.instructions: Match each word to the correct definition.

pairs.definition: one food used to make a dish
pairs.explanation: Ingredients are parts of a recipe.

pairs.definition: an arrangement to keep a table or seat
pairs.explanation: You make a reservation before going to some restaurants.

pairs.definition: the feeling or mood of a place
pairs.explanation: Atmosphere describes how a place feels.

pairs.definition: a choice or liking for one thing more than another
pairs.explanation: Preference explains what someone likes more.

pairs.definition: a special time or event

exercises.title: Restaurant Review Words
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one word from the word bank.

items.sentence: The soup had a rich ______ because it used fresh herbs.
items.explanation: Flavor means the taste of food or drink.

items.sentence: The restaurant had a relaxed and ______ atmosphere.
items.explanation: Casual means relaxed and informal.

items.sentence: The ______ was large enough for two people.
items.explanation: A portion is an amount of food served to one person.

items.sentence: I would ______ this place to anyone who likes traditional food.
items.explanation: Recommend means suggest something because it is good.

items.sentence: My grandmother prefers ______ meals instead of restaurant food.
items.explanation: Homemade means made at home.

exercises.title: MET Personal Experience: Meal
exercises.instructions: Answer in 3–5 sentences. Use at least three target vocabulary words.
exercises.prompt: Describe a memorable meal you had with family or friends. Where were you, and why was it special?
exercises.modelResponse: One memorable meal was a homemade dinner with my family on my birthday. Everyone gathered at my grandmother’s house, and she prepared my favorite dish. The atmosphere was warm and casual, so the occasion felt very special.

modules.title: Environment

targetVocabulary.definition: harmful substances in the air, water, or land
to use old materials again instead of throwing them away
materials that are thrown away
to make something smaller or less serious
to keep something safe from harm
something useful that people can use
the usual weather conditions in a place over time
able to continue without damaging the environment
power used to make things work
to use something carefully so it is not wasted
causing damage or problems
coming from nature, not made by humans
animals and plants living in nature
the natural world around us
a duty or task you are expected to do
harm or injury caused to something
a way to solve a problem
able to be replaced naturally and used again
to use something such as energy, food, or resources
knowledge or understanding of a problem

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary about pollution, energy, and environmental responsibility.
Explain problems and propose solutions.
Build clear MET-style opinions about public/environmental issues.

exercises.title: Environmental Solutions
exercises.instructions: Choose all correct answers. More than one answer may be correct.

items.prompt: Which actions can help protect the environment?
items.explanation: Recycling and conserving water help the environment. Consuming more energy and increasing waste make problems worse.

items.prompt: Which words are usually connected to environmental problems?
items.explanation: Pollution, damage, and waste are problems. Renewable describes energy that can be replaced naturally.

exercises.title: Environment Vocabulary in Context
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one word from the word bank.

items.sentence: Solar and wind power are examples of ______ energy.
items.explanation: Renewable energy can be replaced naturally.

items.sentence: Factories can cause air ______ if they release harmful gases.
items.explanation: Pollution means harmful substances in air, water, or land.

items.sentence: Schools can raise environmental ______ through recycling projects.
items.explanation: Awareness means knowledge or understanding of a problem.

items.sentence: People should ______ water during dry seasons.
items.explanation: Conserve means use carefully so it is not wasted.

items.sentence: A ______ solution protects nature and can continue over time.
items.explanation: Sustainable means able to continue without damaging the environment.

exercises.title: Community Environment Scenario
exercises.instructions: Read the situation and write a response. Use clear, polite, B2-level English.
exercises.scenario: A local park is becoming dirty because visitors leave trash after weekend events.
exercises.task: Write a proposal with one solution and one reason.
exercises.modelResponse: One practical solution is to place more recycling bins around the park and add signs to raise awareness. This could reduce waste because visitors would have a clear and convenient place to throw away bottles and paper. Protecting the park is a shared responsibility for the whole community.
exercises.explanation: The model response gives a clear statement, reason, and support. It also uses topic vocabulary naturally instead of listing words randomly.

modules.title: General Science and Discoveries

targetVocabulary.definition: something new that is found or learned
careful study to discover information
facts or information that show something is true
a test done to learn or prove something
to watch or study something carefully
an explanation based on evidence and study
to study something carefully to understand it
information collected for study or analysis
a repeated form, behavior, or result
the way a person, animal, or thing acts
to grow, change, or improve over time
a new machine, tool, or method
to examine something carefully to find the truth
to make something clear or easy to understand
what happens because of an action or process
a series of steps or changes
correct and exact
important or large enough to notice
having many connected parts; not simple
to say what will probably happen in the future

learningObjectives: Use general science vocabulary in non-specialist contexts.
Identify evidence, results, processes, and patterns.
Practice reading-style vocabulary in context.

exercises.title: Science Vocabulary Match
exercises.instructions: Match each word to the correct definition.

pairs.definition: facts that show something is true
pairs.explanation: Evidence supports an idea or conclusion.

pairs.definition: a test done to learn or prove something
to study carefully to understand
pairs.explanation: Scientists analyze data after collecting it.

pairs.definition: a repeated form, behavior, or result
pairs.explanation: A pattern can show how something usually happens.

pairs.definition: to say what will probably happen
pairs.explanation: Scientists predict based on evidence and data.

exercises.title: Research Words in Context
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer.

items.prompt: The researchers collected ______ from 500 participants.
items.explanation: Data is information collected for study or analysis.

items.prompt: The results showed a ______ improvement in memory after the training.
items.explanation: Significant means important or large enough to notice.

items.prompt: Scientists need to ______ the cause of the problem before suggesting a solution.
items.explanation: Investigate means examine carefully to find the truth.

exercises.title: Science Meaning Check
exercises.instructions: Decide if each statement is true or false. Then explain your answer in one short sentence.

items.statement: A theory is always just a random guess with no evidence.
items.modelExplanation: A theory should be based on evidence and study.
items.explanation: False. In science, a theory is an explanation supported by evidence.

items.statement: If something is complex, it has many connected parts.
items.modelExplanation: Complex means not simple; it has many parts.
items.explanation: Correct.

items.statement: To observe means to ignore something.
items.modelExplanation: Observe means watch or study carefully.
items.explanation: False. Observe means pay attention carefully.

modules.title: Customer Service and Shopping

targetVocabulary.definition: something that you buy
a person who buys something from a store or business
help or work provided for someone
money returned to a customer
to return something and get another item instead
a paper or digital record showing that you paid
something made or sold by a company
how good or bad something is
a lower price than usual
to tell people about a product or service to make them buy it
to say that something is wrong or unsatisfactory
a statement that something is wrong or bad
ready to be used, bought, or chosen
to suggest something because it is good
to look at things to see similarities and differences
not too expensive
useful or worth a lot
to bring something to a person or place
a promise that something will work or be repaired
happy because something was good enough

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary for shopping, complaints, refunds, and service.
Understand common customer-service scenarios.
Practice polite problem-solving language.

exercises.title: Customer Service in Context
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one word from the word bank.

items.sentence: The customer asked for a ______ because the product was broken.
items.explanation: A refund is money returned to a customer.

items.sentence: You usually need a ______ to return an item to the store.
items.explanation: A receipt is proof that you paid.

items.sentence: The phone comes with a one-year ______.
items.explanation: A guarantee is a promise that something will work or be repaired.

items.sentence: The manager listened carefully to the customer’s ______.
items.explanation: A complaint is a statement that something is wrong.

items.sentence: Most customers were ______ with the fast delivery.
items.explanation: Satisfied means happy because something was good enough.

exercises.title: Polite Complaint Scenario
exercises.instructions: Read the situation and write a response. Use clear, polite, B2-level English.
exercises.scenario: You bought a pair of shoes online, but they arrived in the wrong size. You still have the receipt.
exercises.task: Write a polite message asking for a solution.
exercises.modelResponse: Hello, I recently made a purchase from your website, but the shoes were delivered in the wrong size. I have the receipt and would like to request an exchange or a refund. Could you please let me know what procedure I should follow?
exercises.explanation: The model response gives a clear statement, reason, and support. It also uses topic vocabulary naturally instead of listing words randomly.

exercises.title: Shopping Quick Recall
exercises.instructions: Answer each item quickly. Suggested time: 20 seconds per item.

items.prompt: Money returned to a customer:
items.explanation: A refund is money returned.

items.prompt: A lower price than usual:
items.explanation: A discount is a price reduction.

items.prompt: Something a company sells:
items.explanation: A product is something made or sold.

items.prompt: Not too expensive:
items.explanation: Affordable means reasonably priced.

modules.title: Travel and Culture

targetVocabulary.definition: the place where someone is going
a trip from one place to another
something that happens to you and affects you
to travel around a place to learn about it
the customs, beliefs, art, and way of life of a group of people
a custom or habit passed from one generation to another
related to a particular area or community
from another country
traditional ways of behaving in a society
a famous building or place
a place where travelers stay
a plan for a trip, including places and times
full of too many people or things
easy to remember because it was special
easy and useful for a situation
to suggest something because it is good
to look at things to see similarities and differences
to understand the value of something
a special event with music, food, or cultural activities
to change your behavior to fit a new situation

learningObjectives: Use vocabulary about travel, culture, and experiences.
Compare cultural habits and travel preferences.
Describe memorable experiences with clear details.

exercises.title: Travel Vocabulary in Context
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer.

items.prompt: The museum is the most famous ______ in the city.
items.explanation: A landmark is a famous place or building.

items.prompt: Our ______ includes two museums, a local market, and a boat tour.
items.explanation: An itinerary is a plan for a trip.

items.prompt: Travelers often need to ______ to different customs.
items.explanation: Adapt means change your behavior to fit a new situation.

exercises.title: Travel Experience Paragraph
exercises.instructions: Put the sentences in the best logical order.

items.prompt: Order this short travel paragraph.

sentences.text: For example, I tried local food and learned about traditional music.
The trip helped me appreciate a different culture.
Last year, I visited a small city that became a memorable destination for me.
Because of this experience, I became more open to foreign customs.

items.explanation: The paragraph starts with context, then main idea, example, and conclusion.

exercises.title: MET Personal Experience: Travel and Culture
exercises.instructions: Answer in 3–5 sentences. Use at least three target vocabulary words.
exercises.prompt: Describe a place you would recommend to a visitor. Explain what makes it interesting.
exercises.modelResponse: I would recommend my city’s central market because it is a great place to explore local culture. Visitors can try traditional food, talk to local people, and see a famous landmark nearby. The experience is memorable because it shows everyday life, not only tourist attractions.

modules.title: MET Speaking — Picture Description (Task 1)
learningObjectives: Describe a scene or situation using specific, organised language.
Speculate about what is happening and why.
Use MET Task 1 structure: describe → interpret → connect to topic.

exercises.title: Picture 1 — Ski Resort
exercises.imageAlt: A busy ski resort with people on the slopes and a lodge in the background
exercises.prompt: Look at the picture. Describe what you can see, what the people might be doing, and why someone might choose to spend time in a place like this.

structure: In this picture, I can see…
I think this place is… because…

exercises.title: Picture 2 — Airport Check-in
exercises.imageAlt: People queuing at an airport check-in desk with luggage
exercises.prompt: Look at the picture. Describe what is happening, how the people might be feeling, and talk about a time you or someone you know experienced a situation like this.

structure: The people look as if they are…

exercises.title: Picture 3 — Beach Cleanup
exercises.imageAlt: Volunteers collecting rubbish on a beach
exercises.prompt: Look at the picture. Describe what the people are doing and explain why activities like this are important. Do you think individuals or governments should be responsible for protecting the environment?

structure: In this image, I can see people who are…
In my opinion, … should be responsible because…

exercises.title: Picture 4 — City Café
exercises.imageAlt: People sitting in a busy city café, some working on laptops
exercises.prompt: Look at the picture. Describe the scene and the different things people are doing. Would you prefer to work or study in a place like this, or do you prefer somewhere quieter? Give reasons for your answer.

structure: The picture shows a… where people are…
Some people appear to be… while others are…
Personally, I would / would not prefer this because…
Compared to a quiet place, a café…

modules.title: Science and Research

targetVocabulary.definition: something new that is found or learned
careful study to learn new information
facts or information that show something is true
a test done to learn or prove something
to watch or study something carefully
an explanation based on evidence and study
to study something carefully to understand it
information collected for study or analysis
a repeated form, behavior, or result
the way a person, animal, or thing acts
to grow, change, or improve over time
a new machine, tool, or method
to examine something carefully to find the truth
to make something clear or easy to understand
what happens because of an action or process
a series of steps or changes
correct and exact
important or large enough to notice
having many connected parts; not simple
to say what will probably happen in the future

learningObjectives: Use B1-B2 science and research vocabulary accurately in context.
Understand how key terms relate to the scientific process.
Apply target words in MET-style reading and opinion tasks.

exercises.title: Science Words in Context
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one word from the word bank.

items.sentence: The scientists ran an ______ to test whether sunlight affects plant growth.
items.explanation: An experiment is a test done to learn or prove something.

items.sentence: The researchers could not ______ exactly when the volcano would erupt.
items.explanation: 'Predict' means to say what will probably happen in the future.

items.sentence: The study found a ______ improvement in students who slept more than eight hours.
items.explanation: 'Significant' means important or large enough to notice.

items.sentence: You need ______ measurements in science — even small errors change the results.
items.explanation: 'Accurate' means correct and exact.

items.sentence: Scientists need ______ before they can accept a new theory.
items.explanation: Evidence means facts or information that show something is true.

exercises.title: Choose the Right Word
exercises.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence.

items.prompt: Researchers ______ the birds for six months and wrote detailed notes about their habits.
items.explanation: 'Observed' means to watch or study something carefully, which matches 'wrote detailed notes about their habits'.

items.prompt: The ______ showed a clear link between diet and energy levels.
items.explanation: Data means information collected for study or analysis — data can show a link or pattern.

items.prompt: The human brain is ______ — scientists are still learning how it works.
items.explanation: 'Complex' means having many connected parts; not simple — this fits a brain that is not fully understood.

items.prompt: Scientists ______ the causes of the disease for several years before finding a treatment.
items.explanation: 'Investigated' fits because the scientists were examining the disease carefully to find the truth over time.

items.prompt: The ______ of the printing press changed how information was shared across the world.
items.explanation: The printing press was created — it is an invention (a new machine, tool, or method).

exercises.title: Match Word to Definition
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left to its correct definition on the right.

pairs.definition: an explanation based on evidence and study
a repeated form, behavior, or result
what happens because of an action or process
a series of steps or changes
the way a person, animal, or thing acts
to study something carefully to understand it
to grow, change, or improve over time
careful study to learn new information

exercises.title: Your Opinion: Should Science Be Taught Differently?
exercises.prompt: Some people think science should be taught mostly through experiments and real-world investigations. Others think it is better to study theory and data in class first. Which approach do you think is better, and why? Use at least four words from this module in your answer.
exercises.instructions: Write 4–6 sentences. Give your opinion, one reason, and one example. Use at least four words from this module.
exercises.modelResponse: I think students learn science better through experiments rather than only studying theory. When you do an experiment yourself, you can observe the results directly and analyze what happened. This makes the data feel real, not just numbers in a book. For example, in my school we investigated how temperature affects plant growth — the results were surprising and I still remember them. I believe this process of doing and discovering is more significant than memorizing definitions.
scoringFocus: Clear opinion with reason and example
Correct use of at least four module words
